title: descurainia pinnata subsp. nelsonii
intro: Descurainia pinnata subsp. nelsonii, also known as Richardson's wallflower or Nelson's wallflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Descurainia pinnata subsp. nelsonii include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, or used as a tincture to extract its medicinal compounds.
